info.title: CUIT SaaS API
info.description: The CUIT data plane API. Customer recorders POST captured sessions; the warehouse auto-generates regression specs; insights expose flake rate and similar-session search. Used by the @cuit-saas/mcp MCP server, the @cuit/cli (future), and direct REST integrations.

servers.description: Pilot — LIVE today on Fly.io (IAD) + Neon (us-east-1)
Production — sprint week 8 (AWS ECS Fargate + Aurora)

tags.name: admin
tags.description: Tenant + token management. Admin or tenant-bearer auth.

tags.name: instrument
tags.description: App-shape detection and instrumentation proposals (powers /cuit-instrument skill)

tags.name: billing
tags.description: Usage stats and Stripe subscription details

tags.name: public
tags.description: Unauthenticated endpoints — no bearer token required

bearerAuth.description: Per-tenant bearer token. Issued by the cuit-saas admin during onboarding. Pass as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

adminAuth.description: CUIT admin bearer token. Issued by the CUIT operations team for tenant management. Customers do not have this.

token.description: Plaintext bearer token. Shown ONCE; we store only a hash. Save it now.

sessionId.description: Existing session UUID OR session payload below.

allOf.description: When outcome=red: the harness primitive whose assertion failed.

last_snapshot.description: When outcome=red: the state snapshot at the moment of failure.

preceding_events.description: When outcome=red: the last N events before failure for context.

token.description: Plaintext bearer token. Shown ONCE; store it now.

requested_slug.description: Lowercase slug for your tenant. Must start with a letter.

session_id.description: sessions.id UUID. Mutually exclusive with session.

session.description: Inline session payload. Mutually exclusive with session_id.

target_url.description: URL the spec runner will navigate to.

get.description: Returns 200 OK if the API process is alive. No auth required.

post.description: Accepts a SessionEvent[] captured by the recorder. Auto-generates a regression spec when feasible (rule-based today; LLM pipeline ships sprint week 5). Idempotent on (tenant_id, session_id).

201.description: Session stored. Spec may have been generated.

get.summary: List sessions for the authenticated tenant
get.description: Returns up to `limit` most-recent sessions ordered by created_at DESC.

get.summary: List generated specs for the authenticated tenant
get.description: Returns up to 100 most-recent specs ordered by created_at DESC.

get.summary: Per-spec flake rate over the last 28 days
get.description: Returns up to 20 specs ordered by flake rate descending. A spec with `reds`/`runs` close to 1.0 is highly unstable.

post.description: Admin-only. Issues an initial bearer token returned in the response. Token is shown ONCE; we store only its hash. Customer onboarding flow uses this endpoint.

post.summary: Issue a new bearer token for the current tenant
post.description: Used to rotate tokens or to issue scoped tokens for specific environments (CI vs. dev). Token shown ONCE in the response.

get.description: Returns the tenant resolved from the bearer token. Useful for the dashboard.

post.summary: Generate a spec synchronously from a session payload OR session id
post.description: POST /v1/sessions auto-generates a spec asynchronously. Use this endpoint when you want the spec back in the same HTTP response — typical for CI integrations or the /cuit-loop skill. Calls the LLM 3-pass pipeline (Haiku normalize → Sonnet ground → Opus materialize) with prompt caching; falls back to rule-based when model_tier=rule-based.

422.description: Generator could not produce a confident spec from this session

get.summary: Fetch one spec by id (with full primitives + source)

post.summary: Run a spec against a customer URL; returns RED or GREEN with step-level annotations
post.description: The runner spins up a vitest-in-process worker (or headless Playwright per `browser`), executes the spec, and returns the structured outcome. On RED: the response includes `failed_at_primitive`, `last_snapshot`, and the preceding event window so an agent can reason about the failure without re-reading the spec source.

get.summary: List runs across all specs for this tenant
get.description: Filterable by `spec_id`, `outcome`, `since`, `until`. Default: last 100 runs across the tenant.

get.summary: Find sessions vector-similar to this one
get.description: Backed by pgvector cosine similarity over per-session embeddings (voyage-3-lite). The MCP tool `cuit__find_similar_sessions` is a thin wrapper around this endpoint.

get.summary: Aggregate bug-class stats by component over a time window

post.summary: Detect customer app shape (framework, state libs, selectors)
post.description: Used by the `/cuit-instrument` skill via the `cuit__detect_app_shape` MCP tool. Returns the structured shape so the proposal step can target the right files.

package_json.description: Contents of the customer repo package.json

file_tree.description: Flat list of source file paths.

sample_files.description: Optional map of path → content for AST hints.

post.summary: Given an app shape, propose the instrumentation diff
post.description: Used by the `/cuit-instrument` skill via `cuit__propose_instrumentation`. The returned operations[] is consumed by Claude Code's Edit/Write tools to apply the diff.

get.summary: Stream audit log as CSV / JSON / NDJSON
get.description: Compliance export. Returns a streaming response in the requested format. For large windows, prefer NDJSON for line-by-line consumption.
Returns sessions, specs, runs, inference tokens, and estimated cost for the current billing period.

get.summary: Stripe subscription details for the current tenant
get.description: Returns subscription status + a link to the Stripe customer portal for self-service.

delete.description: Hard-deletes sessions, specs, runs, signals, audit log for the authenticated tenant. Asynchronous: completes within 30 days. Returns a purge job id.

post.description: Stripe webhook receiver. NOT bearer-authenticated — signature verified via HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body against STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET. Routes customer.subscription.created/deleted and invoice.payment_failed events to tenant updates and audit log entries.

schema.description: Stripe-Signature header set by Stripe on every webhook delivery. Required for HMAC-SHA256 verification.

200.description: Event received and queued for processing

400.description: Missing or invalid Stripe-Signature header, or webhook misconfigured

post.description: No authentication required. Creates a tenant (tier=team) and returns an initial bearer token in a single round-trip. Rate-limited to 3 signups per IP per rolling hour. The token is shown ONCE in the response — store it securely. PII (email, IP) is stored server-side for compliance and is never echoed back.

201.description: Tenant created. Contains the tenant object and a one-time-visible bearer token.

400.description: Invalid payload (missing fields, bad slug format, etc.)

409.description: Requested slug (and up to 9 auto-suffixed variants) are all taken

429.description: Rate limit exceeded — more than 3 signups from this IP in the past hour

get.summary: Fetch the full events array for a single session
get.description: Returns the JSONB events column for a session. This is the AX-compliant drill-down counterpart to GET /v1/sessions, which returns only compact id+summary rows to stay within token budget (ADR-011 Rule 2). Pass the returned events to cuit__close_loop to generate and run a regression spec.

200.description: AX ok envelope with full events array

post.summary: One-shot loop close: generate spec → run spec → persist trace
post.description: ADR-011 Rule 6 (one-shot composition) + Rule 7 (self-measuring). Runs generate→run→report in ONE call inside a single DB transaction (spec row + run row + loop_trace row + audit row). Returns the loop AX envelope with outcome green|red|error and a trace {turns_to_green, tool_calls, tokens, wall_ms}. Always returns HTTP 200 — the envelope carries the outcome. NOTE: not deduplicated in v0 (each call inserts a new spec row; non-destructive).

200.description: Loop close complete. outcome green|red|error in envelope.

422.description: No spec could be generated from the session events
